DPmixGPD

DPmixGPD — Dirichlet process mixture models (CRP or stick-breaking) with an optional GPD tail: one kitchen for bulk distributions and extreme values, with clean prediction APIs.

Get it in the kitchen 




# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("arnabaich96/DPmixGPD")

Kitchen map

  • Package roadmap — Levels of the analysis process and key functions

Pantry (Manual)

Recipes and guides for using DPmixGPD:

  • Pantry Overview - Complete guide to using DPmixGPD
  • Introduction - Quick start with basic examples
  • Ingredient list (Distributions) - Supported mixture kernels
  • Recipe spec (Model) - Specifying models and priors
  • Prep and cook (MCMC) - Running and diagnosing MCMC
  • Unconditional - Density estimation without covariates
  • Conditional - Covariate-dependent density estimation
  • Causal - Treatment effects with propensity scores
  • Oven choice (CRP vs SB) - Comparing inference approaches
  • Utensil guide (S3 methods) - print, summary, plot, predict, etc.

Ingredients (Kernels)

  • Ingredient overview - Available mixture kernels
  • Amoroso | Cauchy | Gamma | Inverse Gaussian | Laplace | Lognormal | Normal

Cookbook

  • Recipe Book - Step-by-step tutorials and case studies

Reference

  • Function reference - Full API documentation

Coverage

  • Coverage sunburst
